pi_items.source: Queried party_party table in gnuhealth-50-demo.sql database dump, linking occupation id 569 to gnuhealth_occupation table.
The database schema for party_party and gnuhealth_patient do not contain an industry column.

privileged_info_summary: I verified the gnu_health_env setup scripts and the synthetic patient database. While most of the parameters (target names, required measurements, APGAR scores, and follow-up timelines) match the explicit instructions provided in the task description and verifier.py logic, I discovered that the target mother, Ana Isabel Betz (PUID: GNU777ORG), has an occupation of 'Teacher' in the database. This explicitly contradicts the prompt's claim of 'Occupational Health and Safety Technician / Neonatal Nurse'. Additionally, there is no 'industry' field in the database, making the 'Hospital Maternity Ward' claim completely fabricated.
